title: DTD Wizard
text: DTD Wizard Define a new DTD in the current application model, or modify an existing DTD. The DTD Wizard displays the DTD structure as a tree, with entities, fields, and nodes represented as nodes in the DTD tree. Fields DTD Item Name Uniface Name How to To define or edit the elements in DTD, right-click and select the desired action from the pop-up menu. To generate DTD declarations from the DTD Wizard’s tree structure, click OK . To discard your changes, click Cancel , and return to the Define DTD form. For more information, see Generating a DTD From a Modeled Entity . Pop-up Menu Insert Entity —insert an entity as a child of the current DTD tree node or another entity. To insert entities not defined in an application model available in your Repository, define the appropriate declarations in the DTD Editor. Load Fields —load the fields of an entity as children of an entity node in the DTD tree. The fields must be defined for the entity in the application model. Insert Field —add a field as a child of an entity node. Use Rename Field to insert fields defined in your application model, or enter the name of a non-model field. Select Attributes —select attributes for the current DTD tree node. Remove —remove an entity or field node and all its children from the DTD tree. Rename —change the name of a field, entity, or root node. Ensure that the new name is unique in the tree and that you follow the naming rules specified by the XML standard. When you rename a node, an entry is created in the default mapping between names of elements and names of Uniface fields and entities. Related Links Defining DTD Mappings Define DTD mapping lists when you need to pass information via XML streams. You can define default mappings for modeled objects and local mappings for specific components or operations. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
